exercises.name: Extended Puppy Pose
exercises.description:
1. Start on your hands and knees on a mat. 

2. Walk your hands out in front of you while keeping your hips high.

3. Drop your forehead down to the mat. Breathe and relax for the desired amount of time.

exercises.name: Extended Puppy Pose with Leg Extended
exercises.alternateName: lats and inner thigh adductor stretch
exercises.description:
1. Start on your hands and knees on the mat, fully extending one leg directly out to the side.

2. Keeping your hips high, reach your hands out in front of you until you can drop your forehead down to touch the mat. 

3. Breathe and relax in this position for the desired amount of time, then do the same on the other side.

exercises.name: EZ Bar Preacher Curl
exercises.description:
1.For this exercise, you’ll be working your biceps. 

2.Ensure that the top of your arms rest flat on the pad. 

3.Make sure that your feet are well positioned on the floor and place your upper arms on top of the pad. 

4. Use your arms to grab the barbell with a supinated grip (palms facing up) at about shoulder width apart or slightly closer from each other.

5.Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

exercises.name: EZ Bar Upright Row
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright while holding an EZ curl bar, palms facing inward.

2.Exhale and use the sides of your shoulders to lift the bar, raising your elbows up and to the side. Keep the bar close to your body as you raise it.

3.Hold the top contracted position for a moment and squeeze the biceps. 

4.Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position.

exercises.name: Farmer Walk
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding dumbbells with your arms straight by your sides.

2.Walk forward as far as you can while holding the dumbbells.

3.Move for a given distance, typically 50-100 feet, as fast as possible.

exercises.name: Fast Feet
exercises.description:
1) For this exercise, have a slightly aggressive position, lean forward with your upper body.

2.) Move your feet in a run on the spot as fast as you can.

exercises.name: Feet Elevated Inverted Rows
exercises.description:
1. Start with your feet in front of the bar, with heels resting on a bench, and body in a straight line.

2. Grip the bar at shoulder-width, and pull your chest up towards the bar while keeping a stiff abdomen.

3. Slowly return down towards the start position and repeat.

4. To make the exercise harder, lower the bar towards the floor.

exercises.name: Fire Hydrant
exercises.description:
1. When performing this exercise, you'll want to isolate the movement from your hip as much as possible. 

2. Keep your abdominal on and stay strong throughout your body as if you're performing a plank.

3. Position yourself on your hands and knees on the ground. This will be your starting position.

4. Keeping the knee in a bent position, abduct the femur, moving your knee away from the midline of the body. Keep your abdominals on so you do not rotate the hips and body to achieve this. 

5. Pause at the top of the motion, and then slowly return to the starting position.

6. Perform this slowly for a number of repetitions, and repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Fire Hydrant Hip Circle
exercises.description:
1. When performing this exercise, you'll be performing 2 different movements throughout one motion that has moved.

2. The first one will be the leg coming up to the side of the body. The second one will be squeezing the leg right behind you.

3. Position yourself on your hands and knees on the ground. This will be your starting position.

4. Keeping the knee in a bent position, abduct the femur, moving your knee away from the midline of the body. Keep the abdominals engaged while moving the leg to prevent the body or pelvis from rotating.

5. Then move backward in a circular motion, slowly return to the starting position.

6. Perform this slowly for a number of repetitions, and repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Fire Hydrant Standing
exercises.description:
1. When performing this movement, you can hold on to something for balance.

2. Put your hands on your hips and move your knee sideways away from your other knee from your hip.

3. Perform this slowly for a number of repetitions, and repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Flat Bench Back Fly
exercises.alternateName: Lying Prone Reverse Fly rear delt
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, ensure that you squeeze your shoulder blades together to raise the dumbbells up. 

2) Keep a soft elbow bend throughout. 

3) Lie down on a bench. Have the dumbbells in each hand with the palms facing each other 

4) Move the weights out and away from each other in an arc motion. 

5) The arms should be elevated until they are parallel to the floor. 

6) Slowly lower the weights back down.

7) Repeat.

exercises.name: Flat Bench Hex Chest Press
exercises.description:
Lie flat on a bench holding hex dumbbells with a neutral grip. 

Lower the weights to your chest, then press them up, focusing on maintaining control and squeezing the chest muscles at the top.

exercises.name: Floor Glute Kickback
exercises.description:
1.Begin on all fours with knees and hands on the floor.

2.Kick one leg back and up with a 90-degree angle at the knee, pushing your foot towards the ceiling.

3.Lower it back down, bringing the knee towards your chest again and repeat.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Floor Windshield Wiper
exercises.description:
1) Lie with back flat on the ground, arms straight on shoulder level, fingers spread out on against the floor. Feet and legs together, legs raised and straight.

2) Bring your legs, in a diagonal position, towards the ground on one side maintaining a contraction in your abs.

3) Bring the legs on the center returning to starting position.

4) Repeat bringing the legs on the other side. Alternate.

exercises.name: Foam Roller 90/90 Thoracic Opener
exercises.description:
Sit with your legs in a 90/90 position and a foam roller next to you. 

Rotate your upper body toward the roller, reaching out with your arm. 

Hold the stretch, then return to neutral and repeat. Switch sides after a few reps.

exercises.name: Foam Roller Abduction
exercises.description:
1.Lie on your side with the roller under your hip, resting on your forearm and hand with your feet off the floor.

2.Pull your body toward your forearm, rolling down along the side of your thigh from your hip to the top of your knee.

3.Push away rolling back up the side of your thigh from your knee to your hip.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Foam Roller Adductor
exercises.description:
1.Lie face down with one leg bent at the knee 90 degrees and the roller under your inner thigh

2.Roll back and forth along your inner thigh from your inside hip to knee and back.

3.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Foam Roller Alternating Chest Press
exercises.description:
1.Lie on a foam roller vertically along the spine, holding the dumbbells with elbows bent, feet flat on the floor.

2.Press one arm up straight over your chest, while the other remain in starting position.

3.Lower your arm returning to starting position while raising the other arm.

4.Repeat and alternate.

exercises.name: Foam Roller Back
exercises.description:
1.Lie on your back with a roller lengthwise along your spine, feet flat, knees bent and arms by your sides.

2.Roll from side to side across your back, keeping your feet flat.

3.Complete required reps.

exercises.name: Foam Roller Calf
exercises.description:
1.Sit with roller under your ankles with your legs straight and your hands behind your buttocks.

2.Raise your hips off the floor and one ankle off the roller, supporting your bodyweight on your hands.

3.Roll up and down your calf from ankle to knee, pushing your body away with your hands.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Foam Roller Calf Smash
exercises.description:
Sit on the floor with one leg extended and place a foam roller under your calf. 

Apply pressure by lifting your hips off the ground and rolling from your ankle to your knee. Focus on releasing tight spots.

exercises.name: Foam Roller Chest
exercises.description:
1.Lie face down with a roller under your upper chest and your arms stretched overhead.

2.Pull your body toward your forearms, rolling down from your upper chest to your mid-abdomen supporting your weight on your toes and forearms.

3.Push away rolling back from your mid-abdomen to your upper chest.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Foam Roller Chest Fly
exercises.description:
1.Lie on a foam roller vertically along the spine, holding the dumbbells with your arms extended, palms facing up, feet flat on the floor.

2.Raise the dumbbells above your chest, until they meet at the middle, arms extended.

3.Lower down the handles returning to starting position. 

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Foam Roller Chest Opener Stretch
exercises.description: Lie on a foam roller lengthwise along your spine. Open your arms wide to the sides, letting your chest stretch. Hold and breathe deeply.

exercises.name: Foam Roller Chest Press
exercises.description:
1.Lie on a foam roller vertically along the spine, holding the dumbbells with elbows bent, feet flat on the floor.

2.Press the dumbbell up until your arms are straight over your chest. 

3.Lower the dumbbell back to shoulder level. 

4.Repeat

exercises.name: Foam Roller Glute
exercises.description:
1.Sit upright on roller with legs straight out in front and your hands on the mat behind your back.

2.Roll back and forth over your buttocks from the top of your hamstrings to your lower back. Bend and straighten your legs as you roll back and forth.

3.Repeat.

exercises.name: Foam Roller Hamstring
exercises.description:
1.Sit with a roller underneath your knees and your hands behind your buttocks.

2.Raise your body off the ground and roll forward.

3.Push your body back away from your hands rolling up from your knee to your buttocks.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Foam Roller Hip Flexor
exercises.alternateName: Foam Roller tensor fasciae latae TFL
exercises.description:
1.Lie face down with a roller at hip level, legs straight with one foot off the floor resting on your forearms.

2.Leaning to the side of the raised leg roll up and down this leg from the top of your hip to your mid-quad.

3.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Foam Roller Hip Internal Rotation (Seated)
exercises.description: Sit on a foam roller with feet wide, then rotate your knees inward to stretch the internal hip rotators.

exercises.name: Foam Roller Hollow Hold
exercises.description:
Lie on your back with a foam roller between your knees and hands. 

Press the roller firmly as you lift your head, shoulders, and legs slightly off the ground. Hold the position.

exercises.name: Foam Roller Lat
exercises.description:
1.Sit, leaning to one side with a roller under the side of your rib cage resting on your forearm with your elbow bent.

2.Roll upward toward your armpit, straightening your arm.

3.Roll back down from your armpit to your rib cage, bending your elbow.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Foam Roller Lower Back
exercises.description:
1.Lie on a roller in your mid-back with hands crossed in front of your chest, feet flat on the floor and knees bent.

2.Push away, rolling down from your mid-back to your buttocks, straightening your legs.

3.Then pull back rolling up from your buttocks to your mid-back.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Foam Roller Modified Tuck Crunch
exercises.description:
Lie on your back, holding a foam roller between your hands and knees. 

Extend one arm and the opposite leg while keeping the roller stable. Alternate sides.
